data.title: NTPC Teams Up With France's EDF for Pumped Storage, Hydro Projects, Renewable Energy Projects
data.description:
State-owned power producer NTPC has partnered with French government-owned electric utility company Electricité de France SA (EDF), to collaborate on various projects, according to a Sunday filing to the Indian bourses.

The two parties will co-develop and operate pumped storage projects hydropower projects, or hydropower projects bundled with other renewable energy projects, and explore opportunities in the distribution business.

Following the signing of the term sheet for the partnership, NTPC and EDF proposed to form a joint venture company with a 50:50 partnership after securing approval from the Government of India.

The joint venture company will undertake projects on its own or may create special-purpose vehicles to undertake such projects within India and its neighboring countries.

NTPC shares were down nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Bybit Closes 'ETH Gap' as Exchange Replenishes $1.4B Hole After Hack
data.description:
Bybit has returned to a 1:1 backing of client assets and has fully closed the “ether gap” it faced after an unprecedented $1.4 billion hack hit the exchange late Friday.

The exchange has received 446,870 ether (ETH), worth $1.23 billion at current prices, through loans, large deposits, and ether purchases in the past two days, on-chain tracking service Lookonchain said in an X post on Monday.

Address activity suggests more than $400 million were purchased through over-the-counter trading, with another $300 million brought directly from exchanges. Nearly $300 million were sought as loans; the rest are from addresses apparently belonging to crypto funds.

ETH prices rose upto 4% over the weekend amid the apparent buying activity, but are down 2% in the past 24 hours as sentiment isn’t fully lifted.

Meanwhile, Bybit said late Sunday that all deposit and withdrawal activity had “fully recovered to normal levels — with total deposits “slightly exceeding” withdrawals as on Saturday in a sign of market confidence.

Friday’s attack targeted one of Bybit’s offline “cold” wallets, which are typically considered secure due to their lack of internet connectivity, in a heist that allowed $1.4 billion in ETH to be withdrawn.

Hackers gained control by exploiting a sophisticated method involving a manipulated user interface (UI) and URL. This allowed the attackers to alter the smart contract logic, redirecting the funds to an unidentified address. The stolen assets were then split across multiple wallets and swapped on decentralized exchanges.

Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT linked the hack to North Korea’s Lazarus Group, a state-sponsored hacking collective notorious for crypto thefts. Lazarus was behind several high-profile crypto attacks, including the $600 million Ronin Network hack in 2022, and a $230 million drain on Indian exchange WazirX in 2024.

data.title: Bybit Fully Replaced $1.4B Ether Gap after the Hack; Handled $6.1B in Withdrawals
data.description:
Bybit, the cryptocurrency exchange hacked last Friday, withstood an outflow of over $6.1 billion over the weekend. However, the exchange’s CEO announced that the platform replaced the $1.4 billion worth of Ether stolen in the attack.

According to DeFiLlama, the total amount of customers’ assets held by Bybit was around $16.9 billion, which dropped to $10.8 billion as of press time. The withdrawal pressure came as hackers managed to drain roughly 70 per cent of the exchange’s clients’ Ether in the attack.

Total value locked and USD inflows in Bybit; Source: DefiLlama

Assurance of Equal Reserves

Bybit’s CEO, Ben Zhou, posted on X (formerly Twitter) that his exchange “has already fully closed the ETH gap,” adding that “Bybit is again back to 100% 1:1 on client assets through Merkle tree.” He further noted that Bybit would soon publish an audited proof-of-reserves report.

Zhou’s confirmation came after blockchain analytics firm Lookonchain estimated that Bybit received 446,870 Ether, worth around $1.23 billion, which was about 88 per cent of the stolen amount, from loans, whale deposits, and purchases.

Out of the total, the hacked exchange bought 157,660 Ether, worth about $437.8 million, from crypto investment firms Galaxy Digital, FalconX, and Wintermute through over-the-counter transactions. The exchange bought another $304 million of Ether from centralised and decentralised exchanges.

Ben Zhou
@benbybit

Latest Update: Bybit has already fully closed the ETH gap, new audited POR report will be published very soon to show that Bybit is again Back to 100% 1:1 on client assets through merkle tree, Stay tuned. https://t.co/QLa1vOujM6

Feb 24, 2025

The Largest Crypto Heist

The attack on Bybit has resulted in the biggest heist from any crypto exchange to date. On-chain analysts linked the attack to North Korea’s notorious Lazarus Group. Bybit also launched a bounty program with $140 million to gather leads on the massive cyberattack.

Although the exchange did not publicly pinpoint the vulnerability that led to the attack, its CEO said, “We know the cause is definitely around the Safe cold wallet. Whether it’s a problem with our laptops or on Safe’s side, we don’t know.”

Safe is a decentralised custody protocol that offers smart contract wallets for managing digital assets. Some exchanges have integrated Safe, enabling users to retain control of their funds while using multi-signature functionality to improve the security of their cold wallets.

Following the Bybit attack, Safe temporarily shut down its smart wallet functionalities, which increased the hacked exchange’s concerns over mounting withdrawal requests. However, it coordinated with Safe and other platforms to establish a smooth process and honour the withdrawal requests.

data.title: Humanoid robots gain attention as AI advances
data.description: Global tech companies are continuously developing humanoid robots as artificial intelligence (AI) technology continues its rapid advacement. Humanoid robotics startup Figure AI released a video on YouTube on Thursday local time, introducing its AI model for robots, called Helix. Helix is a vision language action (VLA) model that enables robots to recognize their surroundings, understand user commands, and take appropriate actions. The video features two of Figure AI’s latest humanoid robots, Figure 02. When a person placed groceries on a shelf and commanded, “Work together and organize them,” the robots observed the items and began sorting them - deciding which should go into the refrigeration and which should stay on the shelf. The robot, positioned in front of the refrigerator, opened the door and neatly stored eggs, cheese, and ketchup inside, while the other robot opened the shelf and placed cookies inside. Although their movements were slower than a human’s, their wrist and arm rotations, as well as their ability to grasp objects with their fingers, appeared impressively natural. They also demonstrated the ability to naturally hand over objects that needed to be placed closer to the other robot. In the video, one robot picked up an apple and passed it to the other, which then placed the apple in a bowl and set it on the shelf. The two robots even made eye contact, as if they were communicating. “Helix is designed to control two robots simultaneously,” Figure AI said. “One robot can assist the other in performing a variety of household tasks.” Founded in 2022, Figure AI initially focused on developing bipedal robots for the logistics and warehouse industries. But with recent technological advancement, the company has expanded its business into household robotics. Its technological prowess has been recognized, attracting investments last year from Microsoft Corp., OpenAI Inc., Nvidia Corp., and Amazon.com Founder Jeff Bezos. According to Bloomberg, Figure AI’s valuation recently surged to $39.5 billion, a more than 15-fold increase than 2024’s $2.6 billion. Norwegian robotics startup 1X Technologies has also unveiled a home-use humanoid robot called NEO Gamma, which is capable of performing household chores. NEO Gamma walks on two legs and can complete various tasks. According to a video released by 1X Technologies, the robot can turn on a coffee maker and deliver coffee, adjust a crooked picture frame on the wall, clean windows and shelves, and operate a vacuum cleaner - all on its own. With AI tech giants increasingly entering the humanoid robotics field, the competition is intensifying. According to Bloomberg, Meta Platforms Inc. recently revealed in an internal memo that it aims to maximize the capabilities of the Llama platform and will focus on research and development related to consumer humanoid robots. It is reportedly forming a new department for this purpose. Meta initially plans to develop its own humanoid robot hardware for household tasks and in the future create related AI, sensors, and software. It has already begun discussions on collaboration with Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics and Figure AI. Meanwhile, Google has invested in robotics company Apptronik, which is considered a competitor to Tesla’s humanoid robot, Optimus. OpenAI also included the robotics sector in its trademark registration filing in January 2025, stating that it aims to develop humanoid robots equipped with communication and learning functions to assist and entertain people.

data.title: Blackyak I&C aims to become industry leader by 2030
data.description: Blackyak I&C Co., a manufacturer of safety footwear, industrial safety apparel and supplies under South Korea’s BYN Blackyak Group, is accelerating efforts to strengthen its position in the Korean workwear market. This market has expanded to a scale of 1 trillion won ($695.97 million) with the enforcement of the country’s Serious Accidents Punishment Act. Blackyak I&C said on Sunday that its workwear brand “Blackyak Workwear” was recently picked up by major distribution channels such as Costco and Traders, signaling its full-scale market expansion. “We are expanding our distribution network to grow not only in the business-to-business (B2B) sector but also in the business-to-consumer (B2C) market,” Blackyak I&C Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Kim Tae-hyo said. “We have selected products such as sneakers, slippers, sandals, and T-shirts that can be comfortably worn for work as well as in everydaylife.” Blackyak I&C has demonstrated solid performance since entering the workwear market in 2019 with the production and sale of safety footwear. It posted an average annual revenue growth of 27 percent and an operating profit margin of 25 percent from 2020 to 2023, with an estimated 7 percent increase in revenue in 2024. The company’s launch of “Blackyak Workwear,” leveraging its technological expertise and brand power, has proven successful in introducing premium safety footwear and apparel. Its key products include the non-slip safety shoes (YAK-405D) and Korea’s first heated vest, which has sold more than 100,000 units. The company also became the first manufacturer in the industry to be listed on the country’s secondary Kosdaq market in January 2025, which gained it further attention. Having experienced rapid growth in a short period of time, Blackyak I&C has positioned itself as a new growth engine for BYN Blackyak Group. The Korean safety equipment market continues to expand due to the rising demand for industrial safety products following the enforcement of the Serious Accidents Punishment Act. The domestic safety equipment market exceeded 1 trillion won for the first time in 2023, reaching 1.03 trillion won, and has been growing at an average annual rate of 6.3 percent. The market is projected to expand to 1.24 trillion won by 2027. The Korean safety equipment market is dominated by three major players: Blackyak I&C, K2 Safety Co., and Ziben Safety Co., with Blackyak I&C ranking third in market share. With the market’s growth and the rising trend of work jackets, fashion and outdoor brands such as fashion group Hyungji, Kolon FnC, and Eider are also entering the market by launching workwear brands one after another, intensifying competition among companies. Blackyak I&C, which raised a large amount of money through its listing, aims to use the funds for technological investments to become the industry leader by 2030. “Our goal is to become the top company in the industry within five years,” Kim said. “We will use the raised funds to build a smart logistics system and expand our fashionable workwear brand ‘WORXONE,’ which was launched in the second half of 2024.” WORXONE has already received a positive response, securing sales contracts with 10 dealerships 미a mere two months after its launch.

data.title: Fried chicken franchise BHC opens 4th U.S. store in San Diego
data.description: BHC, a Korean fried chicken franchise operated by Dining Brands Group Co., announced Monday the opening of its fourth U.S. store in Rancho Bernardo, San Diego. Spanning approximately 120 square meters, the new location primarily offers delivery and take-out services. The store features BHC’s signature menu items, including Bburingkle, Matcho-King, and Gold-King, served in portioned pieces. It also offers platter options, allowing groups of three to five to sample a variety of chicken flavors, along with Korean food offerings such as cheese scallion pancakes. BHC entered the U.S. market in 2023 and has since been expanding its presence by incorporating both its signature and locally tailored menu items. Following the Rancho Bernardo launch, the company plans to open additional locations in Carlsbad and Riverside later this year. BHC operates 29 stores across seven countries, including the U.S., Canada, Hong Kong, and Singapore.

data.title: Far East Forecasts Net Profit for 2024; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Far East is expecting to book a net profit for 2024 compared with a net loss a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

The company has attributed the return to profit to an increase in revenue from its engineering solutions segment.

The company will release its financial figures by March 1.

Shares of the company were up over 3% in recent trading.

data.title: Ganfeng Lithium to Fully Buy Solar Equipment-Making Subsidiary for 200 Million Yuan
data.description:
Ganfeng Lithium Group plans to fully acquire its subsidiary, Shenzhen Yichu Energy Technology, for 200 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Saturday.

Yichu Energy Tech develops and sells photovoltaic and new energy power equipment.

The Chinese lithium producer will buy 90% of Yichu Energy Tech from another subsidiary, Jiangxi Ganfeng Lithium Battery Technology.

The company's shares slid over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: BayWa to Transfer Majority Stake in BayWa re to EIP
data.description:
BayWa plans to carry out a capital increase in BayWa re through a commercial agreement with Energy Infrastructure Partners, or EIP.

EIP will assume a majority stake in BayWa re, while BayWa will relinquish existing claims from shareholder loans to BayWa re worth 350 million euros, according to a Sunday release. BayWa will reduce its stake in BayWa re to 35% from 51% following the capital increase.

German agricultural and industrial goods company BayWa said the reduction in its claims will require adjustments to its restructuring report and an agreement with its two main shareholders. This is also expected to result in its restructuring phase being extended by one year to the end of 2028.

data.title: Indian equity benchmarks at 8-month lows as US growth concerns spook investors
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes fell to their lowest in eight months on Monday, weighed by sharp losses in heavyweight financials and information technology stocks, as concerns over slowing growth in the United States spooked investors.

The Nifty 50 and the BSE Sensex were down 1% each at 22,568.50 and 74601.88 points, respectively, as of 09:46 a.m. IST. They hit their lowest since early June.

The benchmarks have been under pressure for most of February, falling in 15 out of 17 trading sessions, on consistent foreign outflows, uncertainty about U.S. tariff policies and concerns over slowing domestic growth.

The session's drop comes on the back of data on Friday showing that U.S. business activity tumbled in February, indicating that businesses and consumers were becoming increasingly rattled by policies of U.S. President Donald Trump's administration.

Twelve of the 13 major sectors fell on the day, with financials and information technology falling 1.4% and 2.2%, respectively, which analysts said was due to a sell-off from foreign portfolio investors, who have substantial exposure to these sectors.

Two analysts said that slowing growth in the world's largest economy does not bode well for export-driven sectors such as the information technology due to their high revenue contribution from the U.S.

The index was the top percentage loser of the day.

Indian equities were sharply underperforming Asian peers, most of which were also trading lower.

The underperformance was mainly due to underlying weak sentiment due to concerns over tapering off of earnings growth, Sunny Agrawal, equity analyst at SBICAPS Securities, said.

Meanwhile, the pharma index was the only sector in green, finding its footing after sharp losses last week due to fears of U.S. tariffs on the sector.

Healthcare Global Enterprises rose 3.5% on private equity firm KKR's $400 million stake-purchase plan.

The broader midcap and smallcap indexes were down 1.2% each.

data.title: Soccer-Liverpool's Slot stays cautious despite 11-point lead in title race
data.description:
Liverpool manager Arne Slot cautioned against complacency despite his side opening up an 11-point lead in the Premier League with Sunday's 2-0 win at Manchester City.

Liverpool are on 64 points from 27 matches, with second-placed Arsenal, who have played a game less, on 53 points after Saturday's 1-0 home loss to West Ham United.

"In every other league, having a lead like this would be very comfortable, except for this one," Slot told reporters on Sunday.

Every game presented the potential for a slip-up, he said, pointing to Liverpool's difficult 2-1 home win against relegation-threatened Wolverhampton Wanderers last week.

"We are in a good position but we also know how hard it is to play against Wolves," he added.

Slot expects another tough game on Wednesday against fifth-placed Newcastle United, who drew with Liverpool in December and are coming off a 4-3 win over Nottingham Forest.

"Now we play Newcastle, we played 3-3 over there, which in my opinion was also a good result ... but we also experienced how difficult that one is," he said.

"We work every single day to achieve this and it is three months of very hard work to maintain this. There is no secret. We are 11 points clear but Arsenal has one game in hand.”

data.title: U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield's Downside Risk Increasing, Charts Show — Market Talk
data.description:
0529 GMT - The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield's downside risk is increasing, says Quek Ser Leang of UOB Global Economics & Markets Research in a research report. The yield decisively broke below the bottom of the daily Ichimoku cloud last Friday, the markets strategist notes. This break, together with the weekly moving average convergence divergence indicator turning negative, suggests rising downside risk in the 10-year yield. The level to watch is 4.335%, the top of the weekly Ichimoku cloud, followed by the 55-week exponential moving average, which is now at 4.265%, he adds. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield is little changed at 4.4196%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0422 GMT - Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - China's official manufacturing PMI likely indicated another contraction in February with a 49.8 reading, Moody's Analytics says in a note. "Even as production chugs along to fulfill existing orders, a further 10% tariff in the U.S. on goods from China should hit new orders," it says. China's official gauge of factory activity contracted to 49.1 in January from December's 50.1 as factories suspended operations days ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday. As for the non-manufacturing PMI, retail sales should get a boost from Lunar New Year activity but construction will likely stay in the doldrums, it says. China's manufacturing PMI data is due Saturday. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0338 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session, driven by risk-on sentiment fueled by gains in U.S. stock-index futures and projected results from Germany's election. Initial estimates showed the CDU and its CSU sister party securing just under 29% of the vote, followed by Alternative for Germany, or AfD, with around 20%. Given that the election outcome aligns with expectations, analysts at Maybank say there are hopes of reduced political uncertainty. Additionally, signs of fading U.S. exceptionalism have emerged, supported by recent economic data. USD/SGD is 0.3% lower at 1.3319. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0222 GMT - More upside for JGB yields and the yen looks likely, Capital Economics' John Higgins says in a commentary. Japanese inflation and PMI data released Friday bolster Capital Economics' view that the JGB 10-year yield will continue to rise, the chief markets economist says. The ongoing rise in JGB yields probably stems from investors' reassessment of the Bank of Japan's monetary-policy outlook, Higgins says. The BOJ's policy rate is likely to reach 1.25% by end-2026, which feeds into Capital Economics' forecast for JGB 10-year yield to end 2025 at 1.75%. This could also buoy the yen against the dollar, says Capital Economics, which forecasts USD/JPY to reach 145 by end-2025. USD/JPY is 0.1% higher at 149.46. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Singapore's CPI likely rose 1.95% on year in January, according to the median estimate of six economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. That would compare with December's 1.6% increase. Headline inflation is expected to pick up due to a higher contribution from accommodation costs and private road transport, Sanjay Mathur, chief economist for Southeast Asia and India at ANZ, says in a note. Core inflation is projected to have eased to 1.6% last month from December's 1.8% rise, according to five of the economists polled. Core inflation will likely continue declining, partly due to last year's higher base from the GST hike, ANZ says. The data are due Monday. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0123 GMT - Asian currencies strengthen against the U.S. dollar in early trade amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would decrease the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Also, "playing to the emerging theme of a loss of U.S. exceptionalism, other services PMIs around the globe showed improvements," the senior FX strategist says. The weak U.S. data led to overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril adds. USD/KRW falls 0.2% to 1,431.80; AUD/USD edges 0.1% higher to 0.6369. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0035 GMT - Euro rises against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digest the outcome of Germany's election. Exit polls and early vote-counting suggest the Christian Democratic Union won around 29% of votes and will probably lead a coalition government, CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team say in a research report. "However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," the team says. Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team adds. EUR/USD rises 0.3% to 1.0499; EUR/JPY gains 0.2% to 156.42. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls 0.6% to 2638.50 in early trade as semiconductor and steel stocks retreat. Foreign and institutional investors are net sellers, as Wall Street's Friday falls are weighing on sentiment. Memory-chip makers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix decline 1.4% and 3.3%, respectively. Chip-making equipment supplier Hanmi Semiconductor loses 4.6%. Steelmaker Dongguk Steel slides 3.1%, paring Friday's sharp gains fueled by Seoul's move to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,436.50 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 3.2 bps at 2.856%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: REF Expects Lower Profit in 2024; Shares Slide 3%
data.description:
REF Holdings expects a decrease in profit attributable to the owners of up to 50% in 2024, compared with about HK$11.7 million for the corresponding period of the previous year, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

The company attributed the expected decline in profit to lower revenue, increased share-based payment expenses, and higher credit loss provisions due to share option grants and expected trade receivable losses this year.

The financial printing services business plans to publish its financial results on March 6.

The company's shares were down more than 3% in the recent trade.

data.title: PropNex Subsidiary Faces Legal Action
data.description:
PropNex wholly owned subsidiary, PropNex Realty, is one of three co-defendants in a law suit filed in the high court of Singapore, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Saturday.

The legal actions stems from claims against the subsidiary for alleged breach of duty of care owed to the claimant who is seeking around SG$849,287 in damages.

The company is currently seeking legal advice and has decided to defend the suit.

data.title: Relief as German vote dodges extremes
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Wayne Cole.

The market reaction has been relief at the election of the relatively mainstream CDU/CSU conservatives in Germany's election, with DAX futures now up 1.4% after a slow start. The euro has added 0.5% to a one-month high at $1.0528, clearing the previous top of $1.0514. The next target is $1.0534.

Conservative leader Friedrich Merz still has to form a coalition government and it is not yet clear whether he will need one or multiple partners, with the latter sure to take more time. Analysts assume a straightforward coalition with the SPD would be the most favoured outcome, but there's a lot of horse trading to come first. The German Ifo survey due later could show a pick-up in anticipation of a Merz win.

German leadership is sorely needed given question marks over President Donald Trump's support for NATO and Ukraine. European Union leaders are set to hold an extraordinary summit on March 6 to discuss additional support for Ukraine and how to pay for Europe's newly burgeoning defence requirements. This will almost certainly require more debt issuance and some relaxation of EU budget rules. Maybe they could bring back War, sorry, Defence Bonds to get patriotic retail investors involved.

Across the Atlantic, just the threat of tariffs was enough to slam the services PMI sharply lower and it's likely to get worse with reports the White House is actually pressuring Mexico to raise its own tariffs on Chinese imports.

A jump in U.S. consumer inflation expectations to the highest since 1995 will not be welcomed by Fed policy makers, who have constantly comforted themselves with the assertion that expectations were "well anchored". There are at least nine Fed speakers this week so there's plenty of scope for verbal warnings ahead of the PCE inflation report on Friday.

Wall Street futures have at least bounced in Asian trade, perhaps on hopes Nvidia's results on Wednesday will justify its stratospheric valuation.

Investors are looking for fourth-quarter sales around $38.5 billion and first-quarter guidance around $42.5 billion, while any caution on future AI capex would be a downside risk.

Key developments that could influence markets on Monday:

- German Ifo survey of business for Feb, EU final CPI data

- BoE research conference where Deputy Governors Dave Ramsden and Clare Lombardelli speak, along with Bank of Canada Deputy Governor Toni Gravelle. BoE committee member Swati Dhingra speaks.

data.title: DistIT to Sell Operations in Lithuania for EUR3 Million
data.description:
DistIT's Aurdel Sweden subsidiary agreed to divest its business in Lithuania for 3 million euros, according to a Feb. 21 release.

The Swedish purchaser of distributors of accessories for information technology, mobility, multimedia, network, and data communications is selling its Deltaco Baltic UAB unit to Latvia-based Elko Grupa.

Completion of the transaction is subject to customary regulatory approvals.

data.title: Belimo's FY24 Net Income, Sales Grow
data.description:
Belimo Holding said Monday that its net income and net sales for full-year 2024 rose year over year.

Net income attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 146.8 million francs, compared with 137 million francs a year ago. EPS moved to 11.94 francs from 11.14 francs.

The heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning field devices manufacturer's net sales were 943.9 million francs, compared with 858.8 million francs earlier.

Meanwhile, the board will propose a dividend of 9.50 francs per share at an annual general meeting on March 24, anticipated to be paid on March 28. It paid 8.50 francs per share a year ago.

Belimo expects net sales in local currencies to grow slightly above its five-year average in 2025.

data.title: Acer to Acquire Stakes in Enrich Investment, Embedded City for Up to NT$3.45 Billion
data.description:
Acer's board has approved the acquisition of a 25.6% stake in Enrich Investment and 100% of Embedded City for up to NT$3.45 billion.

The move will strengthen Acer's presence in the AIoT industry and boost its B2B business growth, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The acquisition will be funded by the company's own capital, it said.

data.title: Hon Hai Unit Buys 25% Stake in Goertek Electronics Vietnam for $50 Million
data.description:
Hon Hai Precision Industry's unit, New Wing Interconnect Technology (Bac Giang), acquired a 25% stake in Goertek Electronics Vietnam for $50 million.

The deal is aimed at strategic investment and is subject to regulatory approvals, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: AsiaMedic, Sunway Group Unit Launch Medical Diagnostics Imaging Center in Singapore
data.description:
AsiaMedic and Sunway Equity, a subsidiary of Sunway Group , launched AsiaMedic Sunway Imaging at the Royal Square in Novena, Singapore, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Saturday.

The medical diagnostics imaging center has a total area of around 6,000 square feet and is equipped with advanced medical imaging equipment.

The new center aims to address the growing demand for clinics, hospitals and other healthcare providers, AsiaMedic said.

data.title: BBR Holding (S) Forecasts Substantial Increase in Attributable Profit in 2024
data.description:
BBR Holding (S) is expecting to report a substantial increase in its attributable profit to shareholders in 2024, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

The company has attributed the higher profit to a bargain purchase from the acquisition of JSCL Investments.

The group is expected to release its financial figures on or before Feb. 28.

data.title: AirAsia owner Capital A to publish internal targets to improve transparency
data.description:
Malaysia's Capital A Berhad , owner of budget airline AirAsia, said on Monday it will start to publish internal business targets alongside quarterly results to give investors a better picture of the company's financial outlook.

Capital A was hard hit by pandemic travel restrictions and classified by Malaysia's stock exchange as 'PN17', or financially distressed. It is seeking to exit PN17 status.

It is also in the process of selling its AirAsia aviation business to long-haul unit AirAsia X Bhd , which it announced a year ago in a move to consolidate long and short-haul operations under a single AirAsia brand.

Capital A companies want "to build investor confidence through consistent and reliable financial disclosures while ensuring that all stakeholders have the necessary information for a fair and reasonable valuation of the company", it said.

Publication will begin from Wednesday, the investment holding company said.

"With most of the uncertainties that previously impacted our business now behind us, the timing for this move is right," Group Chief Executive Tony Fernandes said in a statement.

Capital A added that it had made "significant progress" on the AirAsia X Bhd sale, saying all necessary consents are expected by end-February.

In January, it extended the deadline for the disposal by two months to March 24.

AirAsia was founded in 2001 with two aircraft and has since become one of Asia's largest budget airline operators.

Both Capital A and AirAsia X were given PN17 status, under which they could be delisted from the stock exchange if they fail to stabilise their finances within a set time frame. AirAsia X was removed from the classification in November 2023.
